mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when RSAFS growth exceeds 3-month median.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero, signaling extended monetary accommodation. When FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25% for consecutive months, equity risk premiums compress and forward returns turn positive over 20 trading days as investors reach for yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 2+ consecutive months
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above its 3-month trend, signaling expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength. This triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 3-month moving average by > 0.5pp
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with accelerating industrial activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) move together in an expansion phase, jointly signaling broad-based demand recovery. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity valuations expand and S&P 500 forward returns turn positive over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive month-over-month growth in same month
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS grow month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month while unemployment rate remains elevated above 8.5%, signaling labor market weakness that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: weak labor market recovery with persistent unemployment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy (FEDFUNDS < 1%), creating expectations of future rate hikes. This policy-inflation mismatch produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets price in eventual tightening.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances when inflation-policy mismatch is present.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 500K units, signaling weak demand momentum and insufficient economic acceleration. This dual weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.5% AND HOUST < 500K units
regime_hint: subtrend growth regime with weak housing demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances when both GDP growth and housing starts are below thresholds.
